title: Find Beautiful Indices in the Given Array I
content:
You are given a 0-indexed string s, a string a, a string b, and an integer k.

An index i is beautiful if:

• 0 <= i <= s.length - a.length

• s[i..(i + a.length - 1)] == a

• There exists an index j such that:

• 0 <= j <= s.length - b.length

• s[j..(j + b.length - 1)] == b

• |j - i| <= k

Return the array that contains beautiful indices in sorted order from smallest to largest.

Example 1:

Input: s = "isawsquirrelnearmysquirrelhouseohmy", a = "my", b = "squirrel", k = 15
Output: [16,33]
Explanation: There are 2 beautiful indices: [16,33].
    - The index 16 is beautiful as s[16..17] == "my" and there exists an index 4 with s[4..11] == "squirrel" and |16 - 4| Example 2:

Input: s = "abcd", a = "a", b = "a", k = 4
Output: [0]
Explanation: There is 1 beautiful index: [0].
    - The index 0 is beautiful as s[0..0] == "a" and there exists an index 0 with s[0..0] == "a" and |0 - 0|

Constraints:

• 1 <= k <= s.length <= 105

• 1 <= a.length, b.length <= 10

• s, a, and b contain only lowercase English letters.

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution(object):
    def beautifulIndices(self, s, a, b, k):
        """
        :type s: str
        :type a: str
        :type b: str
        :type k: int
        :rtype: List[int]
        """

hints: For each i, you can iterate over all js and determine if i is beautiful or not.

solutions.python:
class Solution(object):
    def beautifulIndices(self, s, a, b, k):
        n = len(s)
        # Find all occurrences of a and b in s
        def find_all(text, pattern):
            res = []
            start = 0
            while True:
                idx = text.find(pattern, start)
                if idx == -1:
                    break
                res.append(idx)
                start = idx + 1
            return res
        a_indices = find_all(s, a)
        b_indices = find_all(s, b)
        # For each i in a_indices, check if any j in b_indices has |i-j| <= k
        import bisect
        result = []
        for i in a_indices:
            # Find b_indices in [i-k, i+k]
            lo = bisect.bisect_left(b_indices, i - k)
            hi = bisect.bisect_right(b_indices, i + k)
            if lo < hi:
                result.append(i)
        return result
solutions.explanation:
Find all occurrences of a and b using str.find(). For each occurrence i of a, binary search in b's occurrences for any j with |i-j| ≤ k.

Time: O(n*(|a|+|b|) + n log n). Space: O(n).
